name: JSON Schema Validation Rules
description: Enforce data integrity in MongoDB by defining JSON Schema validation rules that reject documents not matching your expected structure.

steps.title: Create a collection with schema validation
steps.description: Define a collection with JSON Schema rules that enforce required fields and types.
steps.command: mongosh --eval 'db.createCollection("products", {validator: {$jsonSchema: {bsonType: "object", required: ["name", "price", "category"], properties: {name: {bsonType: "string", description: "must be a string"}, price: {bsonType: "number", minimum: 0, description: "must be a non-negative number"}, category: {enum: ["electronics", "clothing", "food", "books"], description: "must be a valid category"}, inStock: {bsonType: "bool"}}}}})' mydb

steps.title: Test validation with a valid document
steps.description: Insert a document that passes all validation rules.
steps.command: mongosh --eval 'db.products.insertOne({name: "Laptop", price: 999.99, category: "electronics", inStock: true})' mydb

steps.title: Test validation with an invalid document
steps.description: Attempt to insert a document that violates the schema to verify enforcement.
steps.command: mongosh --eval 'try { db.products.insertOne({name: "Bad Product", price: -10, category: "invalid"}); } catch(e) { print("Validation error: " + e.message); }' mydb
steps.tip: The insert will fail with a document validation error showing which rules were violated.

steps.title: Add validation to an existing collection
steps.description: Apply schema validation rules to a collection that already has data.
steps.command: mongosh --eval 'db.runCommand({collMod: "orders", validator: {$jsonSchema: {bsonType: "object", required: ["customerId", "items", "total"], properties: {customerId: {bsonType: "string"}, items: {bsonType: "array", minItems: 1}, total: {bsonType: "number", minimum: 0}}}}, validationLevel: "moderate"})' mydb
steps.tip: validationLevel 'moderate' only validates inserts and updates to documents that already match the schema. Use 'strict' to validate all writes.

steps.title: View current validation rules
steps.description: Inspect the validation rules configured on a collection.

faq.question: What is the difference between strict and moderate validation?
faq.answer: Strict validates all inserts and updates. Moderate only validates documents that already match the schema, allowing existing non-conforming documents to be updated without triggering errors. Use moderate when adding validation to an existing collection with legacy data.

faq.question: Can I use validation with error or warn action?
faq.answer: validationAction 'error' rejects non-conforming documents (default). 'warn' allows the write but logs a warning to the MongoDB log. Use 'warn' during a migration period to identify non-conforming documents without breaking the application.

faq.question: Does schema validation affect read performance?
faq.answer: No, validation only runs on writes (insert, update, replaceOne). Read queries are not affected. The performance impact on writes is minimal since JSON Schema validation is executed in memory.
